verbatim: Normalize every value through corpus lexicon `canonical_key` identity. (merged + built 2026-07-13: 673 cards live ...)

CRITICAL term collision (registry_alias): canonical_key always means the normalize_identity keyspace; graph 'canonical name' (neo4j_writer canonicalize_entity_name / normalize_entity_name) is a DIFFERENT normalizer that diverges on hyphen/underscore/unicode names ('Box2D-Lite' → 'box2d lite' vs 'box2dlite'). A probe must import normalize_identity exactly (same discipline as the entity_id convention in user memory).
required_receipt: probe script: for N sampled card values, normalize_identity(value_surface) == stored canonical_key, exit 0

action: Fold the retrieval-side curated CONCEPT_ALIASES (query_semantics.py:527) into the S5 versioned alias registry by ABSORPTION — do not create a fourth alias store; treat the graph-side entity_aliases.json / canonical_families.json as a distinct sibling keyspace, migrated separately or explicitly scoped out.
where: backend/services/retriever/query_semantics.py:527 (CONCEPT_ALIASES); versioned alias registry (S5, to-be-built); corpus_lexicon; neo4j_writer.py:43,46 (sibling stores, do not merge)
why: curated rules in Python are the hardcoded-topic-rule anti-pattern the invariants ban
feasibility_note: registry_alias 'alias registry' row flags the exact failure mode: THREE curated alias stores already exist plus the machine-built corpus_lexicon; building the registry as a new store instead of absorbing them makes a fifth. 'Curated aliases' itself collides — the identical phrase names the graph-side canonical_families mechanism (neo4j_writer.py:945) in a DIFFERENT normalizer keyspace; this checklist line means the retrieval-side CONCEPT_ALIASES.
required_receipt: grep artifact: CONCEPT_ALIASES literal list removed from query_semantics.py; registry file with version stamp; before/after retrieval probe showing gated consumption through the EXISTING expansion gate (grounded threshold + rejected_expansions)

verbatim: Complete P1.7 vocabulary batching and caching.
action: Live-verify the IN CODE P1.7 vocabulary cache + epoch invalidation (S0), then complete the remainder: gather-before-fanout, per-corpus batching, vector reuse, exact-lookup separation, lexical-only baseline.
where: backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py (cache); corpus_lexicon epoch machinery
why: every later latency/eval number is dirty until the cache is real in production
feasibility_note: Two P1.7 boxes are IN CODE awaiting live verify (critique table); S0 lands them, and the S5 lexicon rematerialization's epoch bump doubles as the invalidation live test (PLAN_CRITIQUE S5 row). Dark-code debt is C3's exact complaint — finished work earning nothing. P1.7 is also a hard prerequisite gate for P2.2 (S10).
required_receipt: live probe: cache hit-rate + correct epoch invalidation on lexicon rebuild, latency delta recorded; remaining P1.7 boxes checked with receipts

verbatim: After the P1.1 baseline and P1.7 latency prerequisites exist, materialize P2.1 chunk-derived associations, usage frames, specificity statistics, and deterministic multi-point concept representations.
action: Materialize the P2.1 fields riding the S5 rollup/rematerialization: chunk-derived associations, usage frames, ONE persisted per-corpus DF/specificity statistic (shared with the S7 threshold rule), and deterministic multi-point concept representations.
where: Mongo corpus_lexicon (corpus_lexicon.v3 contract); parent_chunks (CONCEPT_SOURCE_FIELDS); Qdrant corpus_{cid8}_schemas payloads
why: these are the admission fields P2.2 recall and S7's DF rule both starve without
feasibility_note: Prerequisites now real: P1.1 baseline captured (EVAL_2026-07-13, 5 boxes [x]); P1.7 latency work lands at S0. Term hazards (registry_latent/facet): 'concept contract' REQUIRES the split — universal concept contract = corpus_lexicon entry schema vs Ghost A summary capture contract; 'DF' risks three parallel implementations (p0_5 parent_df, Qdrant IDF, P2.1 fields) — persist ONE stat both S7 and P2.1 consume; semantic_profile is PLAN-ONLY with three name-adjacent existing symbols.
required_receipt: lexicon entries carrying associations/usage_frames/DF fields with provenance class, per-corpus census; epoch bump verified against the S0 cache

verbatim: Optionally materialize `transfer_edge` rows as a pure evidence join: shared principle/mechanism across documents with different central subjects.
action: OWNER DECISION: materialize transfer_edge OR P3.2 bridge cards — not both — as the single persisted form of the evidence join _role_bridge already computes at query time; record the choice and the reuse contract in the ledger.
where: ledger decision + (if built) a new transfer_edge store joining ghost_b_extractions mechanisms across documents
why: two materializations of one join is the explicitly-banned duplicate implementation
feasibility_note: registry_librarian bridge row confirms: P3.2 bridge cards and transfer_edge rows 'both materialize the SAME evidence join _role_bridge computes at query time' — a decision, not a build, is what is missing. Both are optional; deferring costs only query-time compute. Data-dependency: meaningful edges need post-S4/S5 mechanism coverage.
required_receipt: ledger entry: chosen materialization (or 'stay query-time'), with the rejected alternative struck; if built, row counts + spot-checked evidence chains

verbatim: Surface shelf, matched fields, evidence IDs, and rejected seat reasons directly from deterministic diagnostics.
action: Surface shelf role, matched fields, evidence IDs, and rejected-seat reasons from deterministic diagnostics into the trace metadata (extend the dark reading_path diagnostics seed); no LLM in the explain path.
where: shelf_engine diagnostics → chat trace metadata; frontend live-trace tabs consumer
why: rejected-seat reasons are the debugging surface for every future seat-quality complaint
feasibility_note: Same seam as L307; the diagnostics seed exists dark. The 'Local RAG retrieval' DONE-trace metadata already carries gate/evidence-plan diagnostics (user memory: live chat probe), so the transport exists — this is field plumbing, not architecture.
required_receipt: live authed /api/chat SSE probe: trace metadata shows shelf/matched-fields/evidence-IDs/rejected-reasons for a librarian query

verbatim: Measure librarian behavior with P1.1 before enabling optional LLM stages.
action: Build the still-open P1.1 librarian rubric, then run the held-out eval capturing librarian behavior BEFORE any Phase-4 LLM stage is enabled; record the run as the activation gate.
where: run_heldout_eval.py; heldout question set + librarian rubric; docs/baselines EVAL artifacts
why: an unmeasured deterministic baseline makes every LLM 'improvement' unfalsifiable
feasibility_note: P1.1 core is live (5 boxes [x], baselines captured) but the LIBRARIAN rubric is an open P1.1 box (critique table) — this item is blocked on that rubric, not on infrastructure. Also reconcile the heldout 56-vs-58 drift (registry_eval) before stamping the gate run.
required_receipt: EVAL artifact with librarian rubric scores, dated BEFORE any Phase-4 flag flip; referenced by every subsequent Phase-4 receipt

verbatim: Enrich only missing card fields such as problems, risks, and situations, with span/source validation and explicit generated provenance.
action: Build span/source-validated LLM enrichment that fills ONLY empty card fields (problems, risks, situations), stamping each value with provenance class 'generated' under the P2.1 four-class taxonomy; land the taxonomy field first.
where: card_builder.py enrichment stage; Mongo librarian_cards; provenance taxonomy (P2.1, to-be-built)
why: fills the card fields determinism honestly left empty, without corrupting provenance
feasibility_note: Gated twice: the L321 measurement gate and the PLAN-ONLY provenance taxonomy (extracted/derived/attested/generated, registry_extraction provenance row) — enriching before the taxonomy exists creates unlabeled generated data, violating standing rule 2 (no dark fields). Interacts with L274: enrichment must never overwrite a deterministic value, only fill empties. No explicit S-slot in the plan of record; earliest legal is post-S8, natural home alongside S10's generated-representation work.
required_receipt: card-lint: every generated value carries provenance='generated' + span/source validation record; L274 lint still green post-enrichment

verbatim: Use the grounded planner only when deterministic story-to-capability mapping is thin.
action: Wire the grounded_planner behind its P1.2 activation contract: invoked only when the deterministic story→capability mapping is thin (weak-confidence trigger), with lane preservation and lexicon-ID rejection intact.
where: backend/services/retriever/grounded_planner.py; librarian query path; P1.2 activation-block config
why: the planner amplifies recall on thin mappings without becoming a selection authority
feasibility_note: P1.2's own checklist blocks activation until card.v0 + seats + baseline exist — all real after S8, so this is sequence-gated, not blocked. Term hazard (registry_retrieval): bare 'planner' is ambiguous — grounded_planner (LLM) vs the deterministic query planner (QueryPlanV2, query_plan.py); this item means the former. The gate machinery (grounded threshold, rejected_expansions, original-lane protection) already exists — reuse it.
required_receipt: probe pair: thick-mapping query shows planner NOT invoked; thin-mapping query shows invocation logged with trigger reason, lanes preserved, non-lexicon IDs rejected

verbatim: Let the LLM explain seated paths and caveats from card fields without changing final seats.
action: Add the LLM explanation stage that narrates seated paths and caveats from card fields, plus an asserting test that final seats are byte-identical with the explanation stage on vs off.
where: librarian answer assembly; shelf_engine seat output (read-only to the LLM)
why: explanation quality without selection authority — the invariant made user-visible
feasibility_note: Directly operationalizes the non-negotiable invariant 'an LLM may explain an already-seated path but may not add, remove, or protect books'. Architecturally simple because seats are computed before the LLM sees anything; the test is the deliverable.
required_receipt: pytest exit 0: seats identical explanation-on/off; sample explanation traces citing only card fields + evidence IDs

verbatim: Add only the generated P2.2 representation methods after deterministic multi-point recall, storage, latency, and librarian behavior are measured.
action: Add generated P2.2 representation methods only after the S10 pilot has measured deterministic multi-point recall, storage, latency, and librarian behavior on markbuildsbrands_transcripts (per-concept point caps; contamination eval-run check lands with the first representation store).
where: P2.2 representation store (pilot corpus mark only); eval_firewall contamination check; corpus_lexicon multi-point projections
why: generated representations are only justified where measured deterministic recall falls short
feasibility_note: Matches the S10 row exactly ('prereqs now real: P1.1 baseline, P1.7 batching + lexical-only baseline, admission fields'). Term hazard (registry_eval): 'contamination' here = eval-set leakage (eval_firewall), NOT P0.5 facet contamination — do not route facet work through eval_firewall. Sequencing is self-enforcing: P2.2's own prerequisite boxes gate it.
required_receipt: pilot metrics artifact (recall/storage/latency/librarian deltas, deterministic-only) predating any generated-method commit; contamination eval-run check green

verbatim: Given the same candidate pool, indexed artifacts, and policy version, the deterministic seat engine returns the same seats regardless of whether an LLM participated upstream.
action: Build the replay determinism test: identical candidate pool + indexed artifacts + policy version → identical seats with LLM participation upstream toggled on/off; run in CI against the S10 pilot.
where: shelf_engine + materialized candidate pool artifact (L342 seam); shelf_policy version stamp
why: proves the seat engine's determinism survives LLM amplification
feasibility_note: Depends on the L342 materialized-pool artifact existing — the test is trivial once the pool and policy version are persisted per query. Design the pool artifact first or this milestone has nothing to replay against.
required_receipt: CI test exit 0: seat lists byte-identical across LLM-on/LLM-off given identical pool + policy version

verbatim: An LLM may expand the candidate pool, but every newly reachable candidate passes the identical deterministic field/evidence gates and records `candidate_source`, cited concept IDs, and rejected/accepted reasons.
action: Enforce the identical deterministic field/evidence gates on every LLM-expanded candidate, and add candidate_source, cited concept IDs, and accepted/rejected reasons to the seat diagnostics contract (candidate_source is a new PLAN-ONLY field — define it in the diagnostics schema).
where: shelf_engine candidate admission; seat diagnostics schema; grounded_planner output boundary
why: LLM recall expansion stays safe only if expanded candidates face the same gates as everyone
feasibility_note: The gate code path already exists (same gates as deterministic candidates — reuse, don't fork); the new work is the provenance fields. candidate_source has no code counterpart yet; keep its vocabulary aligned with the P2.1 provenance classes rather than inventing a parallel enum.
required_receipt: test: LLM-added candidate failing field/evidence gates is rejected with recorded reason; passing candidate seats with candidate_source + cited concept IDs in diagnostics

verbatim: Final seat selection can be replayed deterministically from the materialized candidate pool and policy version without another LLM call.
action: Persist the materialized candidate pool + policy version per librarian query (or per eval run) and implement a replay entrypoint that reproduces final seats without any LLM call; assert byte-equality.
where: candidate-pool artifact store (Mongo, new); shelf_engine replay entrypoint; shelf_policy version stamp
why: replayability is the audit trail that makes every past seating decision contestable
feasibility_note: Straightforward given the engine is already deterministic (L292 receipt); the design decision is artifact granularity/retention (per-query pools have storage cost — per-eval-run may suffice for the milestone). This artifact is also the substrate for L336.
required_receipt: replay run: seats reproduced byte-identical from a stored pool + policy version with providers disabled; test exit 0

verbatim: LLM use improves recall/explanation quality without becoming selection authority.
action: Fold into the S10 before/after eval and keep as a standing gate: recall/explanation metrics must improve with LLM stages on, while the L336/L342 determinism tests stay green — any LLM path that changes seats is a release blocker.
where: run_heldout_eval.py + librarian rubric; L336/L342 determinism tests in CI
why: the whole Phase-4 bargain: LLMs buy recall and explanation, never selection authority
feasibility_note: This milestone restates the non-negotiable invariant ('embeddings and optional LLMs may generate candidates or enrich fields, but only deterministic evidence-backed policy may grant final document seats') as a measured claim — it is a continuous gate, not one-time work. Scoreable only after the L321 pre-LLM baseline and the P1.1 librarian rubric exist.
required_receipt: recurring eval report: LLM-on vs LLM-off recall/explanation deltas positive (pre-registered thresholds) with determinism tests green in the same run
